challenges.title: Basic HTML Page
challenges.description: Create a complete HTML page with a DOCTYPE declaration, element, with a , and a <body> containing a <p> paragraph. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code: assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should start with a DOCTYPE declaration' assert ' element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should close the element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should close the element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <title> element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should close the element' assert '<body>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <body> element' assert '</body>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <body> element' assert '<p>' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <p> paragraph element' assert '</p>' in html.lower(), 'Should close the <p> element'
hints: Start with <!DOCTYPE html> to declare the document type
The <head> section contains metadata like <title>, while <body> contains visible content

challenges.title: Hello World Page
challenges.description:
Create an HTML page with an

heading that says "Hello World" and a

paragraph below it with any text. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an

heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should close the

heading' assert 'hello world' in html.lower(), 'The h1 should contain the text "Hello World"' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a

paragraph below the heading' assert '

' in html.lower(), 'Should close the

element' assert html.lower().index('

') < html.lower().index('

'), 'The

heading should appear before the

paragraph'
hints:
Use

for the main heading and

for the paragraph
Make sure the heading appears before the paragraph in your HTML
